title: Modelling of the adsorption of pharmaceutically active compounds on carbon-based nanomaterials

evidence.snippet: Adsorption Gibbs free energy values at 298K are negative, suggesting spontaneity in ibuprofen adsorption onto pristine CNMs.

records.notes: Thermodynamic parameters suggest strong interaction with pristine CNMs.

evidence.snippet: Functionalization of CNMs was found to lower the Gibbs free energy of ibuprofen adsorption.

records.notes: Functionalized CNMs showed different adsorption characteristics compared to pristine.

evidence.snippet: Group iv (p<0.05) trends indicate reduced uptake of ibuprofen compared to initial assays.

records.notes: Data suggests reduced efficacy of ibuprofen adsorption over time.

evidence.snippet: Increase in stability of ibuprofen on functionalized CNMs suggests favorable hydrophobic interactions.

records.notes: High stability suggests potential for effective removal applications.

extraction_notes: The study includes multiple formulations with ibuprofen clearly indicated in the text and figures.
